dict.target: 📚 Visit the [Full Guide](https://primary-theme.github.io/) for the complete documentation including theme features, font guides, customization guide and more

dict.source: 🎯 Check out the [Roadmap](https://git.new/primary/project-tracker) to get a view of Primary's Development
dict.target: 🎯 Check out the [Roadmap](https://git.new/primary/project-tracker) to get a view of Primary's Development

dict.source: 📮 Subscribe to [Primary Theme Updates](https://primarytheme.substack.com/subscribe) to get notified of Primary updates, new features, and goodies right in your inbox!
dict.target: 📮 Subscribe to [Primary Theme Updates](https://primarytheme.substack.com/subscribe) to get notified of Primary updates, new features, and goodies right in your inbox!

dict.source: ⚙️ To change fonts, go to Settings → Appearance → Font, and select the font you want to use (must be installed in your system).
dict.target: ⚙️ To change fonts, go to Settings → Appearance → Font, and select the font you want to use (must be installed in your system).

dict.source: 🌿 Primary v1.0 user? Want to use the original Karla font? Read the [Karla Font Guide](https://primary-theme.github.io/guides/font-recommendations/karla/) to learn how to get back into using it.
dict.target: 🌿 Primary v1.0 user? Want to use the original Karla font? Read the [Karla Font Guide](https://primary-theme.github.io/guides/font-recommendations/karla/) to learn how to get back into using it.

dict.source: 🌸 Visit the [Font Recommendation Guide](https://primary-theme.github.io/guides/font-recommendations/overview/) for more curated font options for Primary! The guide includes a setup tutorial.
dict.target: 🌸 Visit the [Font Recommendation Guide](https://primary-theme.github.io/guides/font-recommendations/overview/) for more curated font options for Primary! The guide includes a setup tutorial.

dict.source: Some fonts offer stylistic alternates to glyphs of their fonts. This can be activated via a CSS property called font feature settings. `1` means "feature is turned on", and `0` means "feature is turned off".
dict.target: Some fonts offer stylistic alternates to glyphs of their fonts. This can be activated via a CSS property called font feature settings. `1` means "feature is turned on", and `0` means "feature is turned off".

dict.source:
This setting is quite technical so if you aren't sure how to use it please refer to the [MDN guide on font-feature-settings](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/font-feature-settings).

Not all fonts offer the same stylistic alternates so it's best to check out your font's respective manuals or specifications.
dict.target:
This setting is quite technical so if you aren't sure how to use it please refer to the [MDN guide on font-feature-settings](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/font-feature-settings).

Not all fonts offer the same stylistic alternates so it's best to check out your font's respective manuals or specifications.

dict.source: This section targets interface font sizes, meaning text that is not in the markdown editor. To change the size of the editor text, please use Obsidian's built-in setting. You can find this under Settings → Appearance → Font Size.
dict.target: This section targets interface font sizes, meaning text that is not in the markdown editor. To change the size of the editor text, please use Obsidian's built-in setting. You can find this under Settings → Appearance → Font Size.

dict.source: Acceptable font size unit is only in px.
dict.target: Acceptable font size unit is only in px.

dict.source: ☝️ By default with Inter, the font is a little bit thin. To increase readability, I increased the base font weight for each category by 50. If you decide to use your own font, the options below are good to use if you find the font you're using a bit too thick. You can bring them back down by 50 to use the original defaults weights.
dict.target: ☝️ By default with Inter, the font is a little bit thin. To increase readability, I increased the base font weight for each category by 50. If you decide to use your own font, the options below are good to use if you find the font you're using a bit too thick. You can bring them back down by 50 to use the original defaults weights.

dict.source: This is an addend to Normal Font Weight for certain interface components that Obsidian wanted to turn into bold.
dict.target: This is an addend to Normal Font Weight for certain interface components that Obsidian wanted to turn into bold.

dict.source: Remove Popup and Pop Down Animations
dict.target: Remove Popup and Pop Down Animations

dict.source: This removes the background blur when opening the Settings, Command Palette, Quick Switcher, and other pop up boxes.This is a good option to toggle on if you are experiencing unreconcilable buggy-ness and visual issues with Primary. You may also toggle this on if you don't prefer the background blur, or just need some performance boost (especially for bigger vaults).
dict.target: This removes the background blur when opening the Settings, Command Palette, Quick Switcher, and other pop up boxes.This is a good option to toggle on if you are experiencing unreconcilable buggy-ness and visual issues with Primary. You may also toggle this on if you don't prefer the background blur, or just need some performance boost (especially for bigger vaults).

dict.source: If you want to remove the border, set this to 0px.
dict.target: If you want to remove the border, set this to 0px.

dict.source: The roundness value's order goes top left, top right, bottom right, bottom left.
dict.target: The roundness value's order goes top left, top right, bottom right, bottom left.

dict.source: Adjust your ribbon's width. Unit is in px only.
dict.target: Adjust your ribbon's width. Unit is in px only.

dict.source: Adjust your ribbon's icon size. Unit is in px only.
dict.target: Adjust your ribbon's icon size. Unit is in px only.

dict.source: Adjust the top and bottom space between your ribbon's icons.
dict.target: Adjust the top and bottom space between your ribbon's icons.

dict.source: Sets the size of the grid, dotted grid, or image
dict.target: Sets the size of the grid, dotted grid, or image

dict.source: Editor Background Size for Pattern Background
dict.target: Editor Background Size for Pattern Background

dict.source: Sets the size of the line grid or dot grid
dict.target: Sets the size of the line grid or dot grid

dict.source: Only available for line grid or dot grid background types. Sets the color of the line grid, dot grid. For line grid, it will take the color input and reduce it to 30% opacity.
dict.target: Only available for line grid or dot grid background types. Sets the color of the line grid, dot grid. For line grid, it will take the color input and reduce it to 30% opacity.

dict.source: Hide Full File Header (Shows when Hovered)
dict.target: Hide Full File Header (Shows when Hovered)

dict.source: Hide Title on File Header (Shows when Hovered)
dict.target: Hide Title on File Header (Shows when Hovered)

dict.source: You may use any CSS units like em, rem, ch, px.
dict.target: You may use any CSS units like em, rem, ch, px.

dict.source: Active Line Gutter Number Text Color
dict.target: Active Line Gutter Number Text Color

dict.source: Adds background color to active line.
dict.target: Adds background color to active line.

dict.source: Only works when Active Line Highlighting is turned on.
dict.target: Only works when Active Line Highlighting is turned on.

dict.source: Set this to 0px if you wish to remove the status bar's border. The border width value's order goes top, right, bottom, left. The default value corresponds the roundness for the On Top style. Applying 1 value means that value is applied to all sides.
dict.target: Set this to 0px if you wish to remove the status bar's border. The border width value's order goes top, right, bottom, left. The default value corresponds the roundness for the On Top style. Applying 1 value means that value is applied to all sides.

dict.source: The roundness value's order goes top left, top right, bottom right, bottom left. The default value corresponds the roundness for the On Top style. Applying 1 value means that value is applied to all corners.
dict.target: The roundness value's order goes top left, top right, bottom right, bottom left. The default value corresponds the roundness for the On Top style. Applying 1 value means that value is applied to all corners.

dict.source: Floating Style - Status Bar Opacity at Rest
dict.target: Floating Style - Status Bar Opacity at Rest

dict.source: Sets the opacity of the status bar at rest if Floating Status Bar style is chosen. Range goes from 0 being transparent, until 1 being completely visible.
dict.target: Sets the opacity of the status bar at rest if Floating Status Bar style is chosen. Range goes from 0 being transparent, until 1 being completely visible.

dict.source: Floating Style - Status Bar Opacity on Hover
dict.target: Floating Style - Status Bar Opacity on Hover

dict.source: Sets the opacity of the status bar when hovering it if Floating Status Bar style is chosen. Range goes from 0 being transparent, until 1 being completely visible.
dict.target: Sets the opacity of the status bar when hovering it if Floating Status Bar style is chosen. Range goes from 0 being transparent, until 1 being completely visible.

dict.source: Applied to the background bar and the value bar.
dict.target: Applied to the background bar and the value bar.

dict.source: Takes in the progress bar's height by default, creating rounded corners. If you want straight corners put 0px.
dict.target: Takes in the progress bar's height by default, creating rounded corners. If you want straight corners put 0px.

dict.source: If you want the color for progress bars valued at 100% to be a solid color, set the same color for each of the three below.
dict.target: If you want the color for progress bars valued at 100% to be a solid color, set the same color for each of the three below.

dict.source: Leftmost color in the gradient for value 100%.
dict.target: Leftmost color in the gradient for value 100%.

dict.source: Middle color in the gradient for value 100%
dict.target: Middle color in the gradient for value 100%

dict.source: Rightmost color in the gradient for value 100%
dict.target: Rightmost color in the gradient for value 100%

dict.source: 🎨 Visit the [Header Presets](https://primary-theme.github.io/guides/overview/) section in the Full Guide to import the Settings.
dict.target: 🎨 Visit the [Header Presets](https://primary-theme.github.io/guides/overview/) section in the Full Guide to import the Settings.

dict.source: 📖 The settings are based on CSS styling capabilities.
dict.target: 📖 The settings are based on CSS styling capabilities.

dict.source: You may choose from left, center, or right.
dict.target: You may choose from left, center, or right.

dict.source: Sets the spacing between multiple lines of text of the heading. You can use units such as px, em, rem as well.
dict.target: Sets the spacing between multiple lines of text of the heading. You can use units such as px, em, rem as well.

dict.source: If you add a background color, Obsidian has some spacing at the top for each header. Turning this on will ensure the heading has equal spacing for both top and bottom, aligning it correctly.
dict.target: If you add a background color, Obsidian has some spacing at the top for each header. Turning this on will ensure the heading has equal spacing for both top and bottom, aligning it correctly.

dict.source: You may also have different thickness per side in the order of top, right, bottom, right (i.e. 5px 2px 3px 4px).
dict.target: You may also have different thickness per side in the order of top, right, bottom, right (i.e. 5px 2px 3px 4px).

dict.source: You may choose from solid, dashed, dotted, and double. Same with thickness, you can have different styles per side.
dict.target: You may choose from solid, dashed, dotted, and double. Same with thickness, you can have different styles per side.

dict.source: Add rounding to your headings. Best to use with a background color. If you'd like to have different roundness for each corner, you can set one in the order of top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left (i.e. 4px 2px 6px 3px).
dict.target: Add rounding to your headings. Best to use with a background color. If you'd like to have different roundness for each corner, you can set one in the order of top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left (i.e. 4px 2px 6px 3px).

dict.source: Not natively supported in markdown, but underline can be activated with HTML.
dict.target: Not natively supported in markdown, but underline can be activated with HTML.

dict.source: Not natively supported in markdown, but underline can be activated with HTML. If used, you can set the underline's line opacity from 0% (no line visibility) to 100% (full color visibility). The color is taken from the underline's text color.
dict.target: Not natively supported in markdown, but underline can be activated with HTML. If used, you can set the underline's line opacity from 0% (no line visibility) to 100% (full color visibility). The color is taken from the underline's text color.

dict.source: This changes how far the underline is from the base of the link's letters.
dict.target: This changes how far the underline is from the base of the link's letters.

dict.source: Set from 0% (no underline visibility) to 100% (full underline color visibility). Underline color inherits the Link's color.
dict.target: Set from 0% (no underline visibility) to 100% (full underline color visibility). Underline color inherits the Link's color.

dict.source: Links that appear outside of notes
dict.target: Links that appear outside of notes

dict.source: Reading Mode – Indentation Line Guide Indent
dict.target: Reading Mode – Indentation Line Guide Indent

dict.source: Might require some customization if you change fonts. Unit is in em.
dict.target: Might require some customization if you change fonts. Unit is in em.

dict.source: Live Preview Mode – Indentation Line Guide Indent
dict.target: Live Preview Mode – Indentation Line Guide Indent

dict.source: Source Mode – Indentation Line Guide Indent
dict.target: Source Mode – Indentation Line Guide Indent

dict.source: Text decoration for checked items default at "line-through". If you would like to remove the strikethrough for checked items, put "none".
dict.target: Text decoration for checked items default at "line-through". If you would like to remove the strikethrough for checked items, put "none".

dict.source: Changes the roundness of the checkbox. You may use other units such as %, em, or rem. If you want circular checkboxes, put "100%". If you want squircle checkboxes, put "5px" or "6px". If you want square checkboxes, put "2px" or "0".
dict.target: Changes the roundness of the checkbox. You may use other units such as %, em, or rem. If you want circular checkboxes, put "100%". If you want squircle checkboxes, put "5px" or "6px". If you want square checkboxes, put "2px" or "0".

dict.source: Changes the roundness of the checkbox. You may use other units such as px, em, or rem.
dict.target: Changes the roundness of the checkbox. You may use other units such as px, em, or rem.

dict.source: Text decoration for items marked [-] default at "line-through". If you would like to remove the strikethrough, put "none".
dict.target: Text decoration for items marked [-] default at "line-through". If you would like to remove the strikethrough, put "none".

dict.source: Set border radius size for media (images, audio, video)
dict.target: Set border radius size for media (images, audio, video)

dict.source: 🎨 Visit the [Note Embed Presets](https://primary-theme.github.io/presets/note-embed) in the Full Guide and check if you have any preferred ready-to-go style presets there, or if you want some inspiration on styling your embeds.
dict.target: 🎨 Visit the [Note Embed Presets](https://primary-theme.github.io/presets/note-embed) in the Full Guide and check if you have any preferred ready-to-go style presets there, or if you want some inspiration on styling your embeds.

dict.source: If you embed very long notes, you can change the max height so that each embed is scrollable. You can also set this by line-height using the unit rlh, or other CSS supported units. Recommended values are 20rlh and 40rlh.
dict.target: If you embed very long notes, you can change the max height so that each embed is scrollable. You can also set this by line-height using the unit rlh, or other CSS supported units. Recommended values are 20rlh and 40rlh.

dict.source: Hide the note's title when embedding notes.
dict.target: Hide the note's title when embedding notes.

dict.source: You can use any CSS supported units. By default takes in the editor's font size based in the Obsidian Settings.
dict.target: You can use any CSS supported units. By default takes in the editor's font size based in the Obsidian Settings.

dict.source: Based on CSS. If you want the note embed title to be in all uppercase, put uppercase. Other options include capitalize and lowercase.
dict.target: Based on CSS. If you want the note embed title to be in all uppercase, put uppercase. Other options include capitalize and lowercase.

dict.source: You can use numbers and CSS units.
dict.target: You can use numbers and CSS units.

dict.source: Note Embed Title Bottom Border Width
dict.target: Note Embed Title Bottom Border Width

dict.source: If you'd like to remove the border, set this to 0px.
dict.target: If you'd like to remove the border, set this to 0px.

dict.source: Note Embed Title Bottom Border Style
dict.target: Note Embed Title Bottom Border Style

dict.source: Other options include dashed, dotted, and double.
dict.target: Other options include dashed, dotted, and double.

dict.source: Note Embed Title Bottom Margin Size
dict.target: Note Embed Title Bottom Margin Size

dict.source: You can also have different radiuses per side. Just write them in the CSS format of top left, top right, bottom right, bottom left. i.e. 2px, 8px, 12px, 0px.
dict.target: You can also have different radiuses per side. Just write them in the CSS format of top left, top right, bottom right, bottom left. i.e. 2px, 8px, 12px, 0px.

dict.source: 🎨 Out of the box, the toggles below affect both file explorer and bookmark folders. If you'd like them to be different colors, head down over to the **Bookmarks** section, turn on `Custom Bookmarks Folders` and set your desired colors.
dict.target: 🎨 Out of the box, the toggles below affect both file explorer and bookmark folders. If you'd like them to be different colors, head down over to the **Bookmarks** section, turn on `Custom Bookmarks Folders` and set your desired colors.

dict.source: Toggle using Folders as Collapse Indicators
dict.target: Toggle using Folders as Collapse Indicators

dict.source: Use folders as collapse indicators instead of chevrons.
dict.target: Use folders as collapse indicators instead of chevrons.

dict.source: Adds colors to the name text of the folder. When hovered, folder background is colored.
dict.target: Adds colors to the name text of the folder. When hovered, folder background is colored.

dict.source: Adds color to the collapse indicators of the folders.
dict.target: Adds color to the collapse indicators of the folders.

dict.source: Toggle Colored Folder Colored Indentation Guide
dict.target: Toggle Colored Folder Colored Indentation Guide

dict.source: Colors folder's indentation guide based on parent folder's color.
dict.target: Colors folder's indentation guide based on parent folder's color.

dict.source: Toggle to Inherit Parent Folder Colors
dict.target: Toggle to Inherit Parent Folder Colors

dict.source: The color cycles through up to 12 colors for both top level folders and subfolders by default. If you would like subfolders to be colored based on their top level folder colors, toggle this on.
dict.target: The color cycles through up to 12 colors for both top level folders and subfolders by default. If you would like subfolders to be colored based on their top level folder colors, toggle this on.

dict.source: 🔄 Folder Colors are setup to cycle through up to 12 colors. At default, it cycles through only 6 colors that I've set up to repeat. Feel free to color them as you wish.
dict.target: 🔄 Folder Colors are setup to cycle through up to 12 colors. At default, it cycles through only 6 colors that I've set up to repeat. Feel free to color them as you wish.

dict.source:
🎨 The collapse indicator and indentation guide colors take in the color of the text color. If you don't want the hassle of changing these two other nuances, you can just change the text color.

Same goes for background color when hovered, you can focus on just changing the background color and leave the hovered color and it will adjust accordingly.
dict.target:
🎨 The collapse indicator and indentation guide colors take in the color of the text color. If you don't want the hassle of changing these two other nuances, you can just change the text color.

Same goes for background color when hovered, you can focus on just changing the background color and leave the hovered color and it will adjust accordingly.

dict.source: By default, File Explorer and Bookmarks share the same colors when the colorful folders toggles are turned on. If you'd like a different color for the Bookmarks folders, toggle this on and set your preferred colors below. Just like File Explorer, customization goes up to 12 folders and cycles through them.
dict.target: By default, File Explorer and Bookmarks share the same colors when the colorful folders toggles are turned on. If you'd like a different color for the Bookmarks folders, toggle this on and set your preferred colors below. Just like File Explorer, customization goes up to 12 folders and cycles through them.

dict.source: Color of notes that don't exist (unresolved backlinks)
dict.target: Color of notes that don't exist (unresolved backlinks)

dict.source: Opacity of nodes that don't exist (unresolved backlinks). Use a number that is less than or equal to 1, but more than or equal to 0.
dict.target: Opacity of nodes that don't exist (unresolved backlinks). Use a number that is less than or equal to 1, but more than or equal to 0.

dict.target: Support development by subscribing monthly or making a one-time donation at [Primary's Ko-fi Page](https://ko-fi.com/ceciliamay).

dict.target: 🐛 Submit an Issue or Feature Request!

Visit [Primary's Github Issue Page](https://git.new/primary/obsidian) to submit an issue or feature request.
No Github account? Send me a message through [Twitter](https://x.com/ceciliamay_) or directly via [Email](mailto:contact.ceciliamay@gmail.com)
dict.target:
Visit [Primary's Github Issue Page](https://git.new/primary/obsidian) to submit an issue or feature request.
